## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Body-fill plus precondition alignment for the six non-Windows BUSJ sub-spec stubs, authored natively against the post-AA_ exemplar (BUSJIW). Reads as ABA's mac/linux sibling pace — the same mechanical-translation rigor, but writing into empty stubs rather than translating existing prose. Land only after ABA so the exemplar set (BUSJIW + the three ABA-translated Windows files) is complete and the new style is a settled target.
  
  ## Goal
  Fill body content of BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL natively against the post-AA_ Control Voicings vocabulary (`busc_*`, `bush_*`) and the BUSJIW exemplar's structural shape (sub-step grammar with control verbs, `«VAR»` cross-state names, bounded NOTEs, decomposed `axhog_guarantee`). Align BUSJGB's precondition list to reference `{bust_invigilate_macos}` / `{bust_invigilate_linux}` and add an Assert-admin-posture first step mirroring BUSJGW's.
  
  ## Boundary
  - Files in scope: BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL, BUSJGB.
  - BUSJCM/CL and BUSJIM/IL adopt `busc_*` (bash console verbs); BUSJHM/HL adopt `bush_*` (handbook verbs); mirror BUSJIW's tag distribution.
  - `«VAR»` names align with BUSJIW where shared (e.g., `«ADMIN_SESSION»`); introduce per-platform names where they don't cross over.
  - Caparison-{macos,linux} ceremony shape: admin SSH key-auth precondition (ssh-copy-id is operator-managed per handbook), then idempotent host posture (Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale launchd on Mac; sshd + sleep-target mask + tailscaled on Linux), closing with invigilate-{platform}.
  - Invigilate-{macos,linux}: posture fact lists with read commands + expected values; failure pointers to handbook (operator-managed) vs caparison (caparison-managed).
  - Handbook-{macos,linux}: operator-manual scope per platform — Tailscale install + login, ssh-copy-id first-run setup, sudoers NOPASSWD entries, anything genuinely operator-side.
  - BUSJGB: precondition list references invigilate-{macos,linux}; first step asserts admin posture via invigilate; mirror BUSJGW's pattern.
  - No BUS0 edits — vocabulary already minted in AA_.
  - No bash code edits.
  
  ## Done when
  - All six non-Windows BUSJ files read as siblings of BUSJIW and ABA-translated BUSJCW/HW/GC.
  - BUSJGB precondition list and first step reference invigilate-{macos,linux}.
  - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green.


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢A-AA6 or mount A-

Docket:
  ## Character
  Body-fill plus precondition alignment for the six non-Windows BUSJ sub-spec stubs, authored natively against the post-AA_ exemplar (BUSJIW). Reads as ABA's mac/linux sibling pace — the same mechanical-translation rigor, but writing into empty stubs rather than translating existing prose. Land only after ABA so the exemplar set (BUSJIW + the three ABA-translated Windows files) is complete and the new style is a settled target.
  
  ## Goal
  Fill body content of BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL natively against the post-AA_ Control Voicings vocabulary (`busc_*`, `bush_*`) and the BUSJIW exemplar's structural shape (sub-step grammar with control verbs, `«VAR»` cross-state names, bounded NOTEs, decomposed `axhog_guarantee`). Align BUSJGB's precondition list to reference `{bust_invigilate_macos}` / `{bust_invigilate_linux}` and add an Assert-admin-posture first step mirroring BUSJGW's.
  
  ## Boundary
  - Files in scope: BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL, BUSJGB.
  - BUSJCM/CL and BUSJIM/IL adopt `busc_*` (bash console verbs); BUSJHM/HL adopt `bush_*` (handbook verbs); mirror BUSJIW's tag distribution.
  - `«VAR»` names align with BUSJIW where shared (e.g., `«ADMIN_SESSION»`); introduce per-platform names where they don't cross over.
  - Caparison-{macos,linux} ceremony shape: admin SSH key-auth precondition (ssh-copy-id is operator-managed per handbook), then idempotent host posture (Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale launchd on Mac; sshd + sleep-target mask + tailscaled on Linux), closing with invigilate-{platform}.
  - Invigilate-{macos,linux}: posture fact lists with read commands + expected values; failure pointers to handbook (operator-managed) vs caparison (caparison-managed).
  - Handbook-{macos,linux}: operator-manual scope per platform — Tailscale install + login, ssh-copy-id first-run setup, sudoers NOPASSWD entries, anything genuinely operator-side.
  - BUSJGB: precondition list references invigilate-{macos,linux}; first step asserts admin posture via invigilate; mirror BUSJGW's pattern.
  - No BUS0 edits — vocabulary already minted in AA_.
  - No bash code edits.
  
  ## Done when
  - All six non-Windows BUSJ files read as siblings of BUSJIW and ABA-translated BUSJCW/HW/GC.
  - BUSJGB precondition list and first step reference invigilate-{macos,linux}.
  - `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
7	Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
8	WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
9	integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
10	WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.
11	
12	## Current Concept
13	
14	**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
15	deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
16	its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
17	Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
18	in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.
19	
20	**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**
21	
22	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
23	  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
24	- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
25	  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
26	  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
27	  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
28	  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
29	  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
30	  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.
31	
32	**BUJB constant:**
33	
34	- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
35	  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
36	  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.
37	
38	**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**
39	
40	- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
41	  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
42	  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
43	  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
44	  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
45	  admin-shell operations.
46	- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
47	  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
48	  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
49	  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
50	  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
51	  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
52	  precondition check.
53	- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
54	  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
55	  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
56	  authorized_keys.
57	
58	**Garrison-destructive model:**
59	
60	- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
61	  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
62	- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
63	  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
64	- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
65	  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.
66	
67	**Cross-cutting premises:**
68	
69	- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
70	  it by reference, never duplicates.
71	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
72	  material; operator owns all key administration.
73	
74	**Tabtarget colophons (23 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**
75	
76	- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
77	- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
78	- `buw-jpI[WML]` — Invigilate per platform — 3
79	- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
80	  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
81	- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
82	- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
83	- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
84	- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
85	- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
86	- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3
87	
88	**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**
89	
90	BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
91	implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
92	mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
93	privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
94	distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
95	hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
96	live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.
97	
98	**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**
99	
100	Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
101	admin posture, then post-completion verification.
102	
103	Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):
104	
105	1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
106	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
107	   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
108	   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
109	   process never sees the password.
110	2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
111	   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
112	   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
113	   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
114	3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
115	4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
116	   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
117	5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
118	   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
119	   values.
120	6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
121	   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
122	7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
123	   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
124	   expected phase boundary).
125	
126	Phase 2 (key auth only):
127	
128	8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
129	   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
130	   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
131	   a session.
132	9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
133	
134	Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):
135	
136	10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
137	    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
138	11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
139	12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
140	    (idempotent).
141	13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
142	    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
143	    wrong.
144	
145	**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**
146	
147	Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
148	ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
149	auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.
150	
151	**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**
152	
153	Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
154	systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
155	invigilate-linux post-check.
156	
157	**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**
158	
159	Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
160	fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
161	and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
162	posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
163	remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
164	garrison's precondition check.
165	
166	**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**
167	
168	1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
169	   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
170	2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
171	   only (no password fallback).
172	3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
173	   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
174	   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
175	4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
176	   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
177	5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
178	   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
179	6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
180	   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
181	   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
182	7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.
183	
184	## Heat Sequence
185	
186	**Spec foundation:**
187	- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
188	- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
189	  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
190	  caparison)
191	
192	**Implementation:**
193	- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
194	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
195	  superseded colophons + handbook entries
196	
197	**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
198	- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
199	  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
200	- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
201	  then macOS/Linux)
202	- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
203	  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)
204	
205	**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
206	- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis
207	
208	**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
209	- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
210	- Garrison-WSL exercise
211	- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
212	- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)
213	
214	## Deferred (named for future return)
215	
216	- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
217	  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
218	  only.
219	- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
220	  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
221	- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
222	- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
223	  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
224	- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
225	  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
226	- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
227	- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
228	- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
229	  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
230	  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
231	  caparison phase or sibling verb.
232	- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
233	  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
234	  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
235	  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
236	  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
237	  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
238	  a time, never both running concurrently).
239	- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
240	  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.
241	
242	## Standing Notes
243	
244	- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
245	  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
246	  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
247	  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
248	  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
249	  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
250	  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
251	  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
252	  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
253	  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
254	  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
255	- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
256	  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
257	  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
258	  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
259	  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
260	- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
261	  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
262	  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
263	  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
264	  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
265	  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
266	- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
267	  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
268	  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
269	  privkey-at-rest protection.
270	- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
271	  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
272	  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
273	  precondition.
274	- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
275	  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
276	  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
277	  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
278	- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
279	  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
280	  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
281	  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
282	  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
283	  daemon ever runs concurrently.
284	- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
285	  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
286	  and path construction.
287	- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
288	  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
289	- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
290	  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
291	- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
292	  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.
293	
294	# jjezs_pace ₢A-AA6
295	
296	## Character
297	Body-fill plus precondition alignment for the six non-Windows BUSJ sub-spec stubs, authored natively against the post-AA_ exemplar (BUSJIW). Reads as ABA's mac/linux sibling pace — the same mechanical-translation rigor, but writing into empty stubs rather than translating existing prose. Land only after ABA so the exemplar set (BUSJIW + the three ABA-translated Windows files) is complete and the new style is a settled target.
298	
299	## Goal
300	Fill body content of BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL natively against the post-AA_ Control Voicings vocabulary (`busc_*`, `bush_*`) and the BUSJIW exemplar's structural shape (sub-step grammar with control verbs, `«VAR»` cross-state names, bounded NOTEs, decomposed `axhog_guarantee`). Align BUSJGB's precondition list to reference `{bust_invigilate_macos}` / `{bust_invigilate_linux}` and add an Assert-admin-posture first step mirroring BUSJGW's.
301	
302	## Boundary
303	- Files in scope: BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL, BUSJGB.
304	- BUSJCM/CL and BUSJIM/IL adopt `busc_*` (bash console verbs); BUSJHM/HL adopt `bush_*` (handbook verbs); mirror BUSJIW's tag distribution.
305	- `«VAR»` names align with BUSJIW where shared (e.g., `«ADMIN_SESSION»`); introduce per-platform names where they don't cross over.
306	- Caparison-{macos,linux} ceremony shape: admin SSH key-auth precondition (ssh-copy-id is operator-managed per handbook), then idempotent host posture (Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale launchd on Mac; sshd + sleep-target mask + tailscaled on Linux), closing with invigilate-{platform}.
307	- Invigilate-{macos,linux}: posture fact lists with read commands + expected values; failure pointers to handbook (operator-managed) vs caparison (caparison-managed).
308	- Handbook-{macos,linux}: operator-manual scope per platform — Tailscale install + login, ssh-copy-id first-run setup, sudoers NOPASSWD entries, anything genuinely operator-side.
309	- BUSJGB: precondition list references invigilate-{macos,linux}; first step asserts admin posture via invigilate; mirror BUSJGW's pattern.
310	- No BUS0 edits — vocabulary already minted in AA_.
311	- No bash code edits.
312	
313	## Done when
314	- All six non-Windows BUSJ files read as siblings of BUSJIW and ABA-translated BUSJCW/HW/GC.
315	- BUSJGB precondition list and first step reference invigilate-{macos,linux}.
316	- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green.

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and PowerShell access paths.
Container runtime for release-1 is Docker Desktop for Windows only; the
WSL distro `rbtww-main` consumes Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL
integration rather than running its own dockerd. Native dockerd inside
WSL is named in §Deferred for the shared-kernel-iptables reason.

## Current Concept

**Jurisdiction** is the BUK feature-area for reaching out to remote nodes
deterministically. Logical-only umbrella concept; `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` is
its implementation seat (BCG-compliant module housing all three verbs).
Neither the umbrella nor the implementation module earns AXLA-entity voicing
in BUS0. Architectural spine in BUS0 §Remote Node Access.

**Two regimes (BURN, BURP):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family). Per-node, git-transported, changes
  rarely. Identifier: investiture.
- BURP — privileged + workload credentials. Per-station-user. Three fields:
  `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER`, `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`,
  `BURP_WORKLOAD_KEY_FILE`. Pubkeys derived from privkeys via
  `ssh-keygen -y` at use-time; no separate pubkey paths or inline strings.
  Identifier: investiture — by construction equal to a registered
  investiture. The BURP directory name must match a BURN profile dir;
  the 1:1 correspondence is enforced by file-presence check at load time.

**BUJB constant:**

- `BUJB_workload_user` — project-wide convention name for the workload OS
  user that garrison provisions on every node. Single source of truth.
  Lives in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` as a tinder constant.

**Three verbs (Caparison, Invigilate, Garrison):**

- **Caparison** — admin host posture establishment, per platform
  (Windows/macOS/Linux). Nuclear: every invocation purges and re-establishes
  the caparison-managed posture from scratch — no skip-if-present logic.
  Operator handbook owns security-sensitive edits (UAC-required registry
  keys, Tailscale interactive auth, etc.); caparison owns idempotent
  admin-shell operations.
- **Invigilate** — read-only host posture verification, per platform. Pure
  precondition assertions; no mutations. Each posture fact has a read
  command, expected value, and failure pointer to either the operator
  handbook (for operator-managed posture) or caparison (for
  caparison-managed posture). Single source of truth for "correctly-configured"
  per platform. Used twice: caparison's post-completion check, garrison's
  precondition check.
- **Garrison** — workload account provisioning only. Per shell-letter
  (b/c/w). First step is invigilate-{platform}; failure surfaces immediately.
  Never reads or writes any `sshd_config`; never alters admin
  authorized_keys.

**Garrison-destructive model:**

- Every garrison wipes prior workload state regardless of which investiture
  or operator runs it. Node runtime state belongs to whoever last garrisoned.
- One workload user per node, ever. No conscript / discharge / withdraw /
  vanquish / inventory verbs; garrison subsumes them.
- Admin-side host posture is caparison's scope; the security-sensitive
  remainder is the per-platform handbook's scope.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP reaches
  it by reference, never duplicates.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.

**Tabtarget colophons (23 total; b/c/w shell support, PowerShell deferred):**

- `buw-r[np][lrv]` — BURN/BURP regime config (List/Render/Validate) — 6
- `buw-jpC[WML]` — Caparison per platform (W=Windows; M=macOS; L=Linux) — 3
- `buw-jpI[WML]` — Invigilate per platform — 3
- `buw-jpG[bcw]` — Garrison per shell-letter (b=native bash for Linux/Mac;
  c=Cygwin; w=WSL) — 3
- `buw-jpS` — Privileged SSH (admin pass-through, all platforms) — 1
- `buw-jwk` — Knock (probe workload reachability) — 1
- `buw-jwc` — Run command file as workload (shell determined by garrison) — 1
- `buw-jws` — Interactive SSH as workload — 1
- `buw-hj0` — Handbook jurisdiction top (dispatches to per-platform) — 1
- `buw-hj[WML]` — Handbook per platform — 3

**Operational seat (`bujb_jurisdiction.sh` + `bujb_cli.sh`):**

BCG-compliant module housing caparison, invigilate, and garrison
implementations, hardcoding the three shell-letter→`command=` directive
mappings (b/c/w) used by garrison on the workload account, the workload
privkey destination path on remote per shell-letter, the canonical WSL
distribution name (`rbtww-main`), and the Windows OpenSSH `sshd_config`
hardening directive set used by caparison-windows. SSH transport mechanics
live in code; BURP carries no shell selection.

**Caparison-Windows ceremony (admin posture; Windows OpenSSH host):**

Three-phase: SSH-trust hardening (former fenestrate scope), then post-trust
admin posture, then post-completion verification.

Phase 1 (over the password-or-key admin SSH session):

1. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password`. Steady-state:
   `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE` authenticates by key. First-run: ssh prompts
   on /dev/tty; operator types the Windows admin password once. Caparison
   process never sees the password.
2. Idempotently place admin pubkey (derived from `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`
   via `ssh-keygen -y`) in `administrators_authorized_keys` as a bare entry
   (no `command=`; uniform across shell-letters). Performed before any
   sshd_config change so key auth is available for phase 2's reconnect.
3. `icacls` lockdown on `administrators_authorized_keys`.
4. Write hardened directives to `sshd_config`: `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
   `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`. Idempotent.
5. Verify written state via PowerShell `Get-Content` returning raw bytes,
   then bash-side BCG-compliant matching extracting effective directive
   values.
6. `sshd -t` validates the config (penultimate atomic op of phase 1; a
   malformed config aborts caparison before it could brick running sshd).
7. `Restart-Service sshd` (final atomic op of phase 1; ssh session
   terminates here — bash orchestrator treats the disconnect as the
   expected phase boundary).

Phase 2 (key auth only):

8. Reconnect as `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with
   `PreferredAuthentications=publickey` (no password fallback). Failure
   here surfaces a brick — the operator's manual password no longer opens
   a session.
9. Verify post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.

Phase 3 (post-trust admin posture):

10. Stage `rbtww-main` WSL distribution under admin user via `wsl --import`
    from Ubuntu-24.04 seed. Always purge-and-reimport; no skip-if-present.
11. Apply powercfg sleep/hibernate disable (idempotent).
12. Set Tailscale service StartupType Automatic; start service
    (idempotent).
13. Run invigilate-windows post-completion check; non-zero indicates
    caparison's operations did not take or operator-managed posture is
    wrong.

**Caparison-macOS ceremony (admin posture; macOS host):**

Compact: enable Remote Login (`systemsetup -setremotelogin on`), orchestrate
ssh-copy-id, apply `pmset` power config, ensure Tailscale service
auto-start. Closes with invigilate-macos post-check.

**Caparison-Linux ceremony (admin posture; Linux host):**

Compact: orchestrate ssh-copy-id, mask sleep/suspend/hibernate targets via
systemctl (laptop/desktop hosts only), enable tailscaled. Closes with
invigilate-linux post-check.

**Invigilate ceremony (per platform; read-only):**

Each invigilate-{platform} reads its posture fact list and asserts each
fact matches expected. Failure surfaces a diagnostic naming the wrong fact
and pointing to either the operator handbook (for operator-managed
posture) or caparison (for caparison-managed posture). No mutations, no
remediation. Invocation contexts: caparison's post-completion check,
garrison's precondition check.

**Garrison ceremony (per shell-letter; workload-only):**

1. Invigilate-{platform} precondition check; failure surfaces a missing or
   stale admin posture before any destructive workload work.
2. SSH-as-`BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` with `BURP_PRIVILEGED_KEY_FILE`; key auth
   only (no password fallback).
3. Destroy existing workload user (if present) via `userdel -r` or
   platform equivalent — removes account + home directory; stray files
   outside home are workload's own concern, not garrison's.
4. Create fresh workload user named `BUJB_workload_user` (unprivileged: no
   sudo, no admin group, ssh-only access).
5. Place workload pubkey in workload's authorized_keys with the
   shell-letter's `command=` directive.
6. Copy workload privkey to remote workload account at the path hardcoded
   in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` per shell-letter (so workload can
   authenticate outbound to GitHub).
7. Validate round-trip — SSH as workload + exec no-op + verify exit 0.

## Heat Sequence

**Spec foundation:**
- spec-bus0-jurisdiction — BUS0 rewrite reflecting current concept
- introduce-fenestrate-narrow-garrison — split admin trust + sshd harden
  out of garrison into a uniform fenestrate verb (since broadened to
  caparison)

**Implementation:**
- implement-jurisdiction — four-field BURP, `BURC_WORKLOAD_USER`,
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh` module, fenestrate + garrison ceremonies, cleanup
  superseded colophons + handbook entries

**Caparison/Invigilate restructuring:**
- BUS0 vocabulary + skeleton stubs (BUSJPF→BUSJCW; new BUSJC[ML],
  BUSJI[WML], BUSJH[WML])
- Per-platform caparison and invigilate spec content (Windows depth first,
  then macOS/Linux)
- Bash alignment via comment annotations (no symbol renames; preserves
  grep-archaeology for the upcoming spec/implementation review cycles)

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**
- localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice (Windows host, blocks on implementation):**
- WSL distribution install (`rbtww-main` set as default)
- Garrison-WSL exercise
- Garrison-Cygwin exercise
- Docker Desktop WSL integration enable + verify (no native dockerd)

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **PowerShell garrison + dispatch** — `buw-jpGp` tabtarget + the `p`
  shell-letter mapping in `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Current heat ships b/c/w
  only.
- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
- **Separate workload→GitHub keypair** — current shape reuses workload SSH
  keypair for outbound; tighten to per-purpose separation if security demands.
- **BURP override fields for non-conventional key layouts** — operators with
  centralized SSH key storage, non-`.pub`-suffix layouts.
- **Localhost garrison/dispatch variants** — deferred; remote nodes only.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.
- **Cygwin / WSL inner sshd hardening** — caparison covers Windows OpenSSH
  only. If c/w garrisons ever require their own sshd to be hardened
  programmatically (rather than operator-managed), that is a future
  caparison phase or sibling verb.
- **Native dockerd inside `rbtww-main`** — Docker Desktop and a per-distro
  native daemon cannot safely coexist on the same Windows host: WSL2 distros
  share one network namespace, so two daemons fight for kernel iptables/NAT
  rules. Release-1 ships Docker Desktop only; Desktop's WSL integration
  serves the rbtww-main shells. If isolation needs ever require a native
  daemon, it would be operator-managed exclusive lifecycle (one daemon at
  a time, never both running concurrently).
- **JJK fundus user provisioning under WSL** — fundus is a JJK concept and
  currently deferred; the Windows handbook does not include a fundus phase.

## Standing Notes

- **Caparison-Windows handles first-time admin trust establishment plus the
  broader admin posture (WSL distribution staging, sleep/hibernate disable,
  Tailscale service auto-start).** SSH-trust phases retain the prior
  fenestrate two-phase structure (password fallback on /dev/tty for first
  run; key auth thereafter; sshd restart kills the phase-1 session).
  Operator-manual Windows scope: install OpenSSH server, enable the
  service, set `PasswordAuthentication yes` temporarily, allow the firewall
  port, ensure the admin user has a known password, apply
  security-sensitive registry edits per BUSJHW
  (DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion etc.), install Tailscale + Run-Unattended
  + first auth, configure netplwiz auto-login.
- **Linux/Mac admin trust enters caparison via ssh-copy-id orchestration.**
  Caparison-{linux,macos} wraps the operator-manual ssh-copy-id flow plus
  per-platform host posture (sleep masking, Tailscale service). The
  operator still does the actual ssh-copy-id one-time setup (or equivalent
  pubkey-placement); caparison verifies and applies the rest.
- **Two-phase SSH-trust within caparison-windows is structural, not
  optional.** `Restart-Service sshd` kills its own ssh session, so any
  post-restart verification must happen over a fresh session. Bash
  orchestrator treats the post-restart disconnect as the expected phase
  boundary, not a failure. The post-trust admin posture phases (WSL stage,
  powercfg, Tailscale service) run after the phase-2 reconnect.
- **Encrypted (passphrase-protected) privkeys not supported.** `ssh-keygen -y`
  prompts interactively; caparison and garrison would hang. Operator
  policy: privkeys unencrypted; station-level security covers
  privkey-at-rest protection.
- **Workload pubkey must be pre-registered with GitHub out-of-band.**
  Operator pastes the pubkey into GitHub (deploy key per repo or personal
  SSH key) before garrison runs. Garrison cannot validate this; it is a
  precondition.
- **WSL distribution is hardcoded to `rbtww-main`** in
  `bujb_jurisdiction.sh`. Caparison-Windows stages it; garrison-WSL
  exports it as a seed for the workload's HKCU\\Lxss. Invigilate-windows
  asserts its presence as a posture fact.
- **Docker Desktop is the release-1 container runtime on Windows.** WSL
  shells reach the daemon via Docker Desktop's per-distro WSL integration
  toggle (Settings → Resources → WSL Integration), enabled for `rbtww-main`.
  Cygwin and Windows-side shells reach the same daemon via the default
  Docker context. No separate dockerd inside the WSL distro; only one
  daemon ever runs concurrently.
- **Some `BURP_PRIVILEGED_USER` values may contain spaces** (e.g., Windows
  admin user `b hyslop`); audit shell quoting at every variable expansion
  and path construction.
- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as `100.x.y.z` /
  tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency.
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call.
- `command=` in authorized_keys is OpenSSH-standard (used by gitolite,
  GitHub, rsync, borg); shrinks key blast radius rather than expanding it.

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AA6

## Character
Body-fill plus precondition alignment for the six non-Windows BUSJ sub-spec stubs, authored natively against the post-AA_ exemplar (BUSJIW). Reads as ABA's mac/linux sibling pace — the same mechanical-translation rigor, but writing into empty stubs rather than translating existing prose. Land only after ABA so the exemplar set (BUSJIW + the three ABA-translated Windows files) is complete and the new style is a settled target.

## Goal
Fill body content of BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL natively against the post-AA_ Control Voicings vocabulary (`busc_*`, `bush_*`) and the BUSJIW exemplar's structural shape (sub-step grammar with control verbs, `«VAR»` cross-state names, bounded NOTEs, decomposed `axhog_guarantee`). Align BUSJGB's precondition list to reference `{bust_invigilate_macos}` / `{bust_invigilate_linux}` and add an Assert-admin-posture first step mirroring BUSJGW's.

## Boundary
- Files in scope: BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL, BUSJGB.
- BUSJCM/CL and BUSJIM/IL adopt `busc_*` (bash console verbs); BUSJHM/HL adopt `bush_*` (handbook verbs); mirror BUSJIW's tag distribution.
- `«VAR»` names align with BUSJIW where shared (e.g., `«ADMIN_SESSION»`); introduce per-platform names where they don't cross over.
- Caparison-{macos,linux} ceremony shape: admin SSH key-auth precondition (ssh-copy-id is operator-managed per handbook), then idempotent host posture (Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale launchd on Mac; sshd + sleep-target mask + tailscaled on Linux), closing with invigilate-{platform}.
- Invigilate-{macos,linux}: posture fact lists with read commands + expected values; failure pointers to handbook (operator-managed) vs caparison (caparison-managed).
- Handbook-{macos,linux}: operator-manual scope per platform — Tailscale install + login, ssh-copy-id first-run setup, sudoers NOPASSWD entries, anything genuinely operator-side.
- BUSJGB: precondition list references invigilate-{macos,linux}; first step asserts admin posture via invigilate; mirror BUSJGW's pattern.
- No BUS0 edits — vocabulary already minted in AA_.
- No bash code edits.

## Done when
- All six non-Windows BUSJ files read as siblings of BUSJIW and ABA-translated BUSJCW/HW/GC.
- BUSJGB precondition list and first step reference invigilate-{macos,linux}.
- `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green.

## Mount ₢A-AA6 — caparison-invigilate-mac-linux-content

**Goal in one sentence:** Body-fill the six non-Windows BUSJ stubs (BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL) natively against the post-AA_ Control Voicings vocabulary and BUSJIW exemplar shape, and align BUSJGB's precondition list + admin-posture-assertion first step to reference the new `{bust_invigilate_macos}` / `{bust_invigilate_linux}` files.

**Heat context** — ₣A- (rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures) [racing, 29/36]. The four Windows BUSJ files (BUSJIW/CW/HW/GC) sit as a coherent RBS-disciplined family after ABA. The six mac/linux stubs remain at original stub size, awaiting native authoring against the now-settled exemplar set. This pace was previously attempted at 6940c1b0 against the pre-AA_ prose shape and then additively unwound at 92a9717b when AA_'s vocabulary mint landed concurrently — clean slate now.

**Docket (full)** — Files in scope: BUSJCM, BUSJCL, BUSJIM, BUSJIL, BUSJHM, BUSJHL, BUSJGB. CM/CL/IM/IL adopt `busc_*` (bash console verbs); HM/HL adopt `bush_*` (handbook verbs); mirror BUSJIW's tag distribution. «VAR» names align with BUSJIW where shared (e.g. «ADMIN_SESSION»); per-platform names where they don't cross over. Caparison-{macos,linux} ceremony shape: admin SSH key-auth precondition (ssh-copy-id operator-managed per handbook), idempotent host posture (Remote Login + pmset + Tailscale launchd on Mac; sshd + sleep-target mask + tailscaled on Linux), closes with invigilate-{platform}. Invigilate-{macos,linux}: posture fact lists with read commands + expected values; failure pointers to handbook vs caparison. Handbook-{macos,linux}: operator-manual scope per platform. BUSJGB: precondition list references invigilate-{macos,linux}; first step asserts admin posture mirroring BUSJGW. No BUS0 edits. No bash code edits. **Done when:** all six files read as siblings of BUSJIW + ABA-translated BUSJCW/HW/GC; BUSJGB updated; `tt/buw-st.BukSelfTest.sh` and `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green.

**Name assessment** — silks `caparison-invigilate-mac-linux-content` fits; no rename needed.

**Proposed approach:**
- Lock the style: read BUSJIW (RBS-aligned exemplar at ba0392f8), then BUSJCW (ABA Windows-caparison sibling), BUSJHW (ABA handbook sibling with literal command listings), and BUSJGC (ABA workload sibling). Cross-check against RBSAC/RBSAV.
- Read the six target stubs + BUSJGB to see current shape and the «VAR» repertoire already established.
- Author the six files. Pairing-by-platform-verb is the natural grouping: caparison pair (CM/CL), invigilate pair (IM/IL), handbook pair (HM/HL). Mac/Linux differ in host-posture mechanics but share ceremony shape, so each pair has internal symmetry against a single «VAR» repertoire.
- BUSJGB precondition + first-step alignment is mechanical (mirrors BUSJGW).
- Run both gates.

**Execution strategy** — Mechanical translation against settled exemplars. The docket explicitly frames this as "ABA's mac/linux sibling pace — the same mechanical-translation rigor, but writing into empty stubs rather than translating existing prose." ABA delegated BUSJHW + BUSJGC to two parallel opus subagents working independently against the locked exemplars (BUSJIW + BUSJCW), and the results were strong (~70s wall, concise reports, both gates green). The same pattern fits here: three parallel opus subagents on the three platform pairs (CM/CL, IM/IL, HM/HL), each handed the locked exemplar set and the paired stub files. BUSJGB alignment is small and lands on the main thread after the six files are in. Recommended: **parallel opus subagents per pair + main-thread BUSJGB + main-thread gate run.**

Ready to proceed? Confirm and I'll start by reading the exemplar set + the six stubs + BUSJGB to lock the style brief I'll hand the subagents.

29	
30	//axhoq_precondition
31	{bust_handbook_windows} operator-manual scope is complete -- OpenSSH
32	Server installed and reachable, security-sensitive registry edits
33	applied (`DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion`, `PlatformAoAcOverride`),
34	Tailscale installed + Run-Unattended + first auth, netplwiz
35	auto-login, and {burp_privileged_user} has a known password.
36	Caparison presupposes this scope.
37	
38	//axhoq_precondition
39	{burp_privileged_user} is in the Administrators group;
40	{burp_privileged_key_file} is unencrypted.
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Phase 1 — open admin SSH session*:
44	.. {busc_call}
45	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
46	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey,password` (steady-state: key via
47	{burp_privileged_key_file}; first run: TTY password)
48	.. {busc_store}
49	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` for phase 1 operations
50	.. {busc_fatal}
51	on connection failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
52	
53	//axhos_step
54	. *Phase 1 — derive admin pubkey*:
55	.. {busc_call}
56	`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_privileged_key_file}
57	.. {busc_store}
58	`«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` from stdout
59	.. {busc_fatal}
60	on derivation failure -- privkey encrypted (unsupported) or unreadable
61	
62	//axhos_step
63	. *Phase 1 — install admin pubkey*:
64	.. write `«ADMIN_PUBKEY»` to `administrators_authorized_keys` over
65	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` as a bare key entry (no `command=`)
66	.. nuclear -- file replaced, not merged
67	.. {busc_fatal}
68	on write failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
69	
70	//axhos_step
71	. *Phase 1 — lock down administrators_authorized_keys ACL*:
72	.. {busc_call}
73	`icacls` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- ACL restricted to SYSTEM and
74	Administrators (Windows OpenSSH refuses the file otherwise);
75	idempotent
76	.. {busc_fatal}
77	on `icacls` failure -- phase 2 reconnect would fail
78	
79	//axhos_step
80	. *Phase 1 — write hardened sshd_config directives*:
81	.. write `PubkeyAuthentication yes`, `PasswordAuthentication no`,
82	`PermitEmptyPasswords no` to `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`;
83	idempotent
84	.. {busc_fatal}
85	on write failure -- restart would still serve password auth
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Phase 1 — verify hardened directives took*:
89	.. {busc_call}
90	PowerShell `Get-Content` on `sshd_config` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»`
91	.. {busc_store}
92	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»`
93	.. {busc_require}
94	`«SSHD_CONFIG_BYTES»` parses to `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
95	`PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no` as effective
96	directive values (BCG-compliant matching)
97	.. {busc_fatal}
98	on mismatch -- prior write did not take or stale override shadows
99	
100	//axhos_step
101	. *Phase 1 — validate sshd_config*:
102	.. {busc_call}
103	`sshd -t` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- penultimate atomic op of phase 1
104	.. {busc_store}
105	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»`
106	.. {busc_require}
107	`«SSHD_TEST_RESULT»` is zero
108	.. {busc_fatal}
109	on non-zero -- aborting preserves the running sshd
110	
111	//axhos_step
112	. *Phase 1 — restart sshd*:
113	.. {busc_call}
114	`Restart-Service sshd` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P1»` -- final atomic op
115	of phase 1
116	.. {busc_fatal}
117	on invocation failure (the post-restart disconnect itself is the
118	expected phase boundary)
119	
120	//axhos_step
121	. *Phase 2 — reconnect under key-only auth*:
122	.. {busc_call}
123	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with
124	`PreferredAuthentications=publickey` using {burp_privileged_key_file}
125	.. {busc_store}
126	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` for phase 3 operations
127	.. {busc_fatal}
128	on reconnect failure -- brick (manual password no longer opens a
129	session); recover via operator's local console
130	
131	//axhos_step
132	. *Phase 3 — stage rbtww-main WSL distribution*:
133	.. {busc_call}
134	`wsl.exe --import` from the Ubuntu-24.04 seed over
135	`«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»` to register `rbtww-main` under the admin user
136	(canonical: `BUJB_wsl_distribution` in {bujb_jurisdiction})
137	.. nuclear -- always purge-and-reimport
138	.. {busc_fatal}
139	on import failure -- WSL feature disabled, seed unavailable, or
140	admin user's HKCU\\Lxss state corrupted
141	
142	//axhos_step
143	. *Phase 3 — disable powercfg sleep and hibernate*:
144	.. {busc_call}
145	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0`,
146	`powercfg /change standby-timeout-dc 0`,
147	`powercfg /hibernate off` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
148	.. {busc_fatal}
149	on `powercfg` failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (Modern
150	Standby AoAc override demotes calls to no-ops)
151	
152	//axhos_step
153	. *Phase 3 — set Tailscale service Automatic and running*:
154	.. {busc_call}
155	`Set-Service -Name Tailscale -StartupType Automatic` then
156	`Start-Service -Name Tailscale` over `«ADMIN_SESSION_P2»`; idempotent
157	.. {busc_fatal}
158	on `Set-Service` or `Start-Service` failure --
159	{bust_handbook_windows} scope (install + Run-Unattended + first auth)
160	
161	//axhos_step
162	. *Post-completion check — run invigilate-windows*:
163	.. {busc_call}
164	{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
165	.. {busc_store}
166	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
167	.. {busc_require}
168	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
169	.. {busc_fatal}
170	on non-zero -- invigilate diagnostic surfaced verbatim
171	
172	//axhog_guarantee
173	On success:
174	
175	* Admin SSH is key-only with admin pubkey installed in
176	  `administrators_authorized_keys` under correct ACLs.
177	* `sshd_config` carries `PubkeyAuthentication yes`,
178	  `PasswordAuthentication no`, `PermitEmptyPasswords no`.
179	* Post-restart admin session authenticates by key alone.
180	* `rbtww-main` WSL distribution is registered under the admin user.
181	* `powercfg` sleep and hibernate are disabled.
182	* Tailscale service is Automatic and running.
183	* {bust_invigilate_windows} confirms every posture fact end-to-end.
184	
185	//axhoc_completion
186	Exit 0 when the post-completion {bust_invigilate_windows} passes.
187	Non-zero on any phase-1 op failure (admin SSH, pubkey, ACL,
188	`sshd_config`, `sshd -t`), phase-2 reconnect failure, any phase-3 op
189	failure (`wsl --import`, `powercfg`, Tailscale), or post-completion
190	invigilate failure.
191

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_handbook_windows}
3	
4	NOTE: Operator-manual scope for Windows -- posture {busn_caparison}
5	cannot or should not automate. No remote contact, no profile mutation.
6	
7	NOTE: Steps establish OpenSSH server reachability,
8	security-sensitive registry edits, Tailscale install + Run-Unattended
9	+ first auth, and netplwiz auto-login. Mismatches surface from
10	{bust_invigilate_windows} as failure pointers back to this handbook.
11	
12	NOTE: Steps missing here cause {bust_caparison_windows} to fail at
13	the point that depends on them.
14	
15	The {bust_handbook_windows} procedure:
16	
17	//axhos_step
18	. *Install OpenSSH Server*:
19	.. {bush_show}
20	GUI path: Settings → Apps → Optional features → Add a feature →
21	search "OpenSSH Server" → Install
22	.. Elevated PowerShell equivalent:
23	+
24	----
25	Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0
26	----
27	.. {bush_require}
28	`Get-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`
29	reports `Installed`
30	
31	//axhos_step
32	. *Set sshd to Automatic and start*: from an elevated PowerShell
33	prompt:
34	+
35	----
36	Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic
37	Start-Service -Name sshd
38	----
39	.. {bush_require}
40	`Get-Service sshd` reports `Status` as `Running`
41	
42	//axhos_step
43	. *Open the SSH port through Windows Firewall*: from an elevated
44	PowerShell prompt:
45	+
46	----
47	New-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server (sshd)' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22
48	----
49	.. {bush_show}
50	substitute the non-default port if the BURN profile carries one
51	.. {bush_show}
52	recent Windows builds create this rule automatically during the
53	OpenSSH install -- this step is a verify-and-add fallback
54	.. {bush_require}
55	`Get-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd` returns the rule
56	
57	//axhos_step
58	. *Enable temporary password authentication*:
59	.. open `C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` in an editor running as
60	Administrator
61	.. set `PasswordAuthentication yes`, save
62	.. restart the service:
63	+
64	----
65	Restart-Service sshd
66	----
67	.. {bush_show}
68	intentionally temporary -- {bust_caparison_windows} flips it back
69	to `no` during phase 1; this step makes the first-run TTY password
70	fall-through possible before any key is installed
71	
72	//axhos_step
73	. *Ensure the admin user has a known password*:
74	.. {bush_require}
75	{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group with
76	a password the operator knows
77	.. set or verify via `lusrmgr.msc` (GUI) or from an elevated cmd
78	prompt:
79	+
80	----
81	net user <username> <newpassword>
82	----
83	.. {bush_show}
84	required for caparison's phase-1 password fallback -- operator types
85	it once on `/dev/tty`; caparison itself never handles the password
86	
87	//axhos_step
88	. *Apply security-sensitive registry edits*:
89	.. {bush_warn}
90	each entry requires elevation -- use `reg add` from an elevated cmd
91	prompt or `New-ItemProperty` from an elevated PowerShell prompt
92	.. `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` = `0` (DWORD) under
93	`HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`:
94	+
95	----
96	reg add "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device" /v DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
97	----
98	.. {bush_show}
99	exposes the netplwiz "Users must enter a user name and password"
100	checkbox on Windows 10/11
101	.. `PlatformAoAcOverride` = `0` (DWORD) under
102	`HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power`:
103	+
104	----
105	reg add "HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power" /v PlatformAoAcOverride /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
106	----
107	.. {bush_show}
108	required on Modern Standby (AlwaysOnAlwaysConnected) systems --
109	without it, caparison's `powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0` is
110	silently demoted to a no-op
111	.. {bush_require}
112	both keys present -- asserted by {bust_invigilate_windows}
113	
114	//axhos_step
115	. *Install Tailscale*:
116	.. download the Tailscale for Windows MSI from
117	https://tailscale.com/download
118	.. run the installer
119	.. {bush_require}
120	Windows service named `Tailscale` is registered and a system-tray
121	icon is present
122	
123	//axhos_step
124	. *Toggle Tailscale Run-Unattended*:
125	.. open the Tailscale system-tray icon → Preferences → enable "Run
126	unattended"
127	.. {bush_show}
128	required because the host runs without a console login during
129	automated tests -- {bust_caparison_windows} sets the service
130	`StartupType` to `Automatic`, but useful traffic only flows once
131	Run-Unattended is on
132	
133	//axhos_step
134	. *Authenticate Tailscale to the operator's tailnet*:
135	.. open the Tailscale system-tray icon → Sign in
136	.. complete the browser-based first-time authentication
137	.. {bush_show}
138	binds the host to the tailnet so the BURN profile's tailnet
139	hostname (or `100.x.y.z` address) becomes a stable transport
140	address that persists across reboots
141	
142	//axhos_step
143	. *Configure netplwiz auto-login*:
144	.. {bush_warn}
145	depends on `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` from the registry step
146	above -- without it the checkbox is not visible
147	.. press Windows + R, type `netplwiz`, press Enter
148	.. select {burp_privileged_user} in the User Accounts dialog
149	.. uncheck "Users must enter a user name and password to use this
150	computer"
151	.. click Apply, then enter the admin password twice when prompted
152	.. {bush_show}
153	host now boots straight to a logged-in admin desktop -- the
154	substrate Tailscale Run-Unattended and OpenSSH depend on for
155	unattended operation
156	
157	//axhog_guarantee
158	On completion:
159	
160	* No remote contact occurred and no local file was written by the
161	  handbook itself.
162	* OpenSSH Server is installed, reachable, and reachable through the
163	  firewall with temporary password auth enabled.
164	* {burp_privileged_user} has a known password and Administrators
165	  membership.
166	* `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` and `PlatformAoAcOverride`
167	  registry values are set.
168	* Tailscale is installed, Run-Unattended is on, and the host is
169	  authenticated to the operator's tailnet.
170	* netplwiz auto-login is configured for {burp_privileged_user}.
171	* The operator-managed posture scope that {bust_caparison_windows}
172	  and {bust_invigilate_windows} depend on is established.
173	
174	//axhoc_completion
175	Exit 0 after handbook content is printed. Non-zero only when content
176	cannot be located or rendered.
177

//axhob_operation
{bust_handbook_windows}

NOTE: Operator-manual scope for Windows -- posture {busn_caparison}
cannot or should not automate. No remote contact, no profile mutation.

NOTE: Steps establish OpenSSH server reachability,
security-sensitive registry edits, Tailscale install + Run-Unattended
+ first auth, and netplwiz auto-login. Mismatches surface from
{bust_invigilate_windows} as failure pointers back to this handbook.

NOTE: Steps missing here cause {bust_caparison_windows} to fail at
the point that depends on them.

The {bust_handbook_windows} procedure:

//axhos_step
. *Install OpenSSH Server*:
.. {bush_show}
GUI path: Settings → Apps → Optional features → Add a feature →
search "OpenSSH Server" → Install
.. Elevated PowerShell equivalent:
+
----
Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0
----
.. {bush_require}
`Get-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`
reports `Installed`

//axhos_step
. *Set sshd to Automatic and start*: from an elevated PowerShell
prompt:
+
----
Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic
Start-Service -Name sshd
----
.. {bush_require}
`Get-Service sshd` reports `Status` as `Running`

//axhos_step
. *Open the SSH port through Windows Firewall*: from an elevated
PowerShell prompt:
+
----
New-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server (sshd)' -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22
----
.. {bush_show}
substitute the non-default port if the BURN profile carries one
.. {bush_show}
recent Windows builds create this rule automatically during the
OpenSSH install -- this step is a verify-and-add fallback
.. {bush_require}
`Get-NetFirewallRule -Name sshd` returns the rule

//axhos_step
. *Enable temporary password authentication*:
.. open `C:\ProgramData\ssh\sshd_config` in an editor running as
Administrator
.. set `PasswordAuthentication yes`, save
.. restart the service:
+
----
Restart-Service sshd
----
.. {bush_show}
intentionally temporary -- {bust_caparison_windows} flips it back
to `no` during phase 1; this step makes the first-run TTY password
fall-through possible before any key is installed

//axhos_step
. *Ensure the admin user has a known password*:
.. {bush_require}
{burp_privileged_user} is a member of the Administrators group with
a password the operator knows
.. set or verify via `lusrmgr.msc` (GUI) or from an elevated cmd
prompt:
+
----
net user <username> <newpassword>
----
.. {bush_show}
required for caparison's phase-1 password fallback -- operator types
it once on `/dev/tty`; caparison itself never handles the password

//axhos_step
. *Apply security-sensitive registry edits*:
.. {bush_warn}
each entry requires elevation -- use `reg add` from an elevated cmd
prompt or `New-ItemProperty` from an elevated PowerShell prompt
.. `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` = `0` (DWORD) under
`HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device`:
+
----
reg add "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\PasswordLess\Device" /v DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
----
.. {bush_show}
exposes the netplwiz "Users must enter a user name and password"
checkbox on Windows 10/11
.. `PlatformAoAcOverride` = `0` (DWORD) under
`HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power`:
+
----
reg add "HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Power" /v PlatformAoAcOverride /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f
----
.. {bush_show}
required on Modern Standby (AlwaysOnAlwaysConnected) systems --
without it, caparison's `powercfg /change standby-timeout-ac 0` is
silently demoted to a no-op
.. {bush_require}
both keys present -- asserted by {bust_invigilate_windows}

//axhos_step
. *Install Tailscale*:
.. download the Tailscale for Windows MSI from
https://tailscale.com/download
.. run the installer
.. {bush_require}
Windows service named `Tailscale` is registered and a system-tray
icon is present

//axhos_step
. *Toggle Tailscale Run-Unattended*:
.. open the Tailscale system-tray icon → Preferences → enable "Run
unattended"
.. {bush_show}
required because the host runs without a console login during
automated tests -- {bust_caparison_windows} sets the service
`StartupType` to `Automatic`, but useful traffic only flows once
Run-Unattended is on

//axhos_step
. *Authenticate Tailscale to the operator's tailnet*:
.. open the Tailscale system-tray icon → Sign in
.. complete the browser-based first-time authentication
.. {bush_show}
binds the host to the tailnet so the BURN profile's tailnet
hostname (or `100.x.y.z` address) becomes a stable transport
address that persists across reboots

//axhos_step
. *Configure netplwiz auto-login*:
.. {bush_warn}
depends on `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` from the registry step
above -- without it the checkbox is not visible
.. press Windows + R, type `netplwiz`, press Enter
.. select {burp_privileged_user} in the User Accounts dialog
.. uncheck "Users must enter a user name and password to use this
computer"
.. click Apply, then enter the admin password twice when prompted
.. {bush_show}
host now boots straight to a logged-in admin desktop -- the
substrate Tailscale Run-Unattended and OpenSSH depend on for
unattended operation

//axhog_guarantee
On completion:

* No remote contact occurred and no local file was written by the
  handbook itself.
* OpenSSH Server is installed, reachable, and reachable through the
  firewall with temporary password auth enabled.
* {burp_privileged_user} has a known password and Administrators
  membership.
* `DevicePasswordLessBuildVersion` and `PlatformAoAcOverride`
  registry values are set.
* Tailscale is installed, Run-Unattended is on, and the host is
  authenticated to the operator's tailnet.
* netplwiz auto-login is configured for {burp_privileged_user}.
* The operator-managed posture scope that {bust_caparison_windows}
  and {bust_invigilate_windows} depend on is established.

//axhoc_completion
Exit 0 after handbook content is printed. Non-zero only when content
cannot be located or rendered.

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_garrison_cygwin}
3	
4	NOTE: {busn_garrison} ceremony with Cygwin bash as the workload shell,
5	for Windows nodes reached via Cygwin sshd. Workload-only -- garrison
6	never touches `sshd_config` and never modifies admin authorized_keys.
7	
8	NOTE: Destruction is letter-agnostic via `zbujb_obliterate_workload`
9	in {bujb_jurisdiction} -- the single greppable destroy verb. Covenant
10	spans the Windows SAM entry, `C:\Users\<user>\` profile, the
11	`C:\cygwin64\home\<user>\` Cygwin home, and the WSL-side
12	`/home/<user>` inside `rbtww-main` when present. Each step idempotent
13	on absent state.
14	
15	NOTE: Admin trust is the responsibility of {busn_caparison} on the
16	Windows OpenSSH server; Cygwin sshd hardening (when run alongside
17	Windows OpenSSH) remains operator-manual.
18	
19	The {busn_garrison} operation on Windows via Cygwin:
20	
21	//axhopt_typed_parameter
22	{burp_investiture}
23	
24	//axhoq_precondition
25	A {burp_regime} profile exists at the given {burp_investiture} and its
26	{burn_regime} targets a Windows node with Cygwin sshd installed,
27	reachable as {burp_privileged_user} under key-only auth using
28	{burp_privileged_key_file}.
29	
30	//axhoq_precondition
31	{busn_caparison} has run for this {burp_investiture} -- admin SSH
32	trust established by Windows OpenSSH key-only auth.
33	
34	//axhoq_precondition
35	{burp_workload_key_file} is unencrypted and exists on the operator's
36	station.
37	
38	//axhos_step
39	. *Assert admin posture*:
40	.. {busc_call}
41	{bust_invigilate_windows} for this {burp_investiture}
42	.. {busc_store}
43	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»`
44	.. {busc_require}
45	`«INVIGILATE_RESULT»` is zero
46	.. {busc_fatal}
47	on non-zero -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope (operator-managed) or
48	{bust_caparison_windows} scope (caparison-managed); diagnostic
49	surfaced verbatim
50	
51	//axhos_step
52	. *Open admin SSH session*:
53	.. {busc_call}
54	ssh as {burp_privileged_user} with {burp_privileged_key_file}; key-auth only
55	.. {busc_store}
56	`«ADMIN_SESSION»`
57	.. {busc_fatal}
58	on connection failure -- {bust_caparison_windows} scope
59	
60	//axhos_step
61	. *Obliterate prior workload*:
62	.. {busc_call}
63	`zbujb_obliterate_workload` over `«ADMIN_SESSION»` -- single
64	PowerShell session purges the Windows SAM entry, profile directory,
65	Cygwin home, and (when `rbtww-main` is installed) the WSL-side Linux
66	user
67	.. nuclear -- idempotent on absent state
68	.. {busc_fatal}
69	on PowerShell session failure -- {bust_handbook_windows} scope
70	
71	//axhos_step
72	. *Create workload account*:
73	.. provision {bujb_workload_user} as an unprivileged ssh-only Cygwin
74	account over `«ADMIN_SESSION»`
75	.. {busc_fatal}
76	on Cygwin account-management failure -- prior obliterate left state
77	
78	//axhos_step
79	. *Place workload trust*:
80	.. {busc_call}
81	`ssh-keygen -y` on {burp_workload_key_file}
82	.. {busc_store}
83	`«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` from stdout
84	.. write `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` to the workload's authorized_keys with
85	the shell-letter `c` `command=` directive (canonical:
86	`BUJB_command_c` in {bujb_jurisdiction}):
87	+
88	[listing]
89	----
90	command="C:/cygwin64/bin/bash --login -c \"$SSH_ORIGINAL_COMMAND\"",no-port-forwarding,no-X11-forwarding,no-agent-forwarding
91	----
92	.. {busc_fatal}
93	on key-write failure -- workload account left without trust
94	
95	//axhos_step
96	. *Plant workload key*:
97	.. copy {burp_workload_key_file} to the workload account at the
98	shell-letter `c` destination path (canonical: `BUJB_workload_keypath_c`
99	in {bujb_jurisdiction})
100	.. {busc_fatal}
101	on copy failure -- workload account reachable but key absent
102	
103	//axhos_step
104	. *Validate round-trip*:
105	.. {busc_call}
106	ssh as {bujb_workload_user} with {burp_workload_key_file}; run a
107	no-op command
108	.. {busc_store}
109	`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»`
110	.. {busc_require}
111	`«WORKLOAD_RT_RESULT»` is zero
112	.. {busc_fatal}
113	on non-zero -- workload trust placed but unreachable
114	
115	//axhog_guarantee
116	On success:
117	
118	* Exactly one workload account named {bujb_workload_user} exists on
119	  the node, reachable via Cygwin bash.
120	* Workload authorized_keys carries `«WORKLOAD_PUBKEY»` with the
121	  shell-letter `c` `command=` directive.
122	* {burp_workload_key_file} planted at the shell-letter `c` destination
123	  authenticates the workload round-trip.
124	* Admin posture is unchanged -- no `sshd_config` writes, no admin
125	  authorized_keys writes.
126	
127	//axhoc_completion
128	Exit 0 when the round-trip validation succeeds. Non-zero on
129	invigilate-windows assertion failure, admin SSH failure, obliterate
130	failure, Cygwin account-management failure, key-write failure, or
131	workload round-trip failure.
132
